zh.lifeSummary: 當Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III於1135年出生時，他的父親Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency46歲，母親'Alice' FitzRoy28歲。他和Mathilde de Garlande至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他於1202年登記服兵役。他去世於1204年在دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه易斯坦波爾易斯坦波爾享年69歲，埋葬在土耳其易斯坦波爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III于1135年出生时，他的父亲Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency46岁，母亲'Alice' FitzRoy28岁。他和Mathilde de Garlande至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他于1202年登记服兵役。他去世于1204年在دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه易斯坦波爾易斯坦波爾享年69岁，埋葬在土耳其易斯坦波爾。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Marly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Marsay, see Marcey . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.
French: habitational name from Marcy, the name of several places in various parts of France (e.g. in Aisne), all derived from the Gallo-Roman estate name Marciacum.

variants.name: Marcey
meanings: English:
variant of Mercer , from Middle English mercer, merser, marser ‘textile merchant’, in which final -er has been weakened to -ey.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Marcy in France (Manche).

nameMeanings.name: Mattheus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Matthaeus; variant of Matthias .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

en.lifeSummary: When Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III was born in 1135, his father, Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency, was 46 and his mother, 'Alice' FitzRoy, was 28. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Mathilde de Garlande. He registered for military service in 1202. He died in 1204, in İstanbul, İstanbul, Ottoman Empire, at the age of 69, and was buried in İstanbul, Türkiye.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III est né en 1135, son père, Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency, avait 46 ans et sa mère, 'Alice' FitzRoy, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Mathilde de Garlande. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1202. Il est décédé en 1204, à İstanbul, İstanbul, Empire ottoman, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à İstanbul, Turquie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III 1135 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency 46 und seine Mutter 'Alice' FitzRoy 28. Er hatte mit Mathilde de Garlande mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1202 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1204 im Alter von 69 Jahren in İstanbul, İstanbul, Osmanisches Reich und wurde in İstanbul, Türkei begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III è nato il nel 1135, il suo padre, Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, 'Alice' FitzRoy, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Mathilde de Garlande. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1202. Morì nel 1204, nel İstanbul, İstanbul, Impero ottomano, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a İstanbul, Turchia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly IIIが1135年に生まれた時，父Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorencyは46歳，母'Alice' FitzRoyは28歳であった。彼はMathilde de Garlandeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1202年に兵役登録をした。彼は1204年にオスマン帝国イスタンブルイスタンブルで69歳で死去し，トルコイスタンブルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III(이)가 1135년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 'Alice' FitzRoy(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Mathilde de Garlande(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1202년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1204년 دولتْ علیّه عثمانیّه 이스탠불 이스탠불에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 튀르키예 이스탠불에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III nasceu em 1135, seu pai, Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, 'Alice' FitzRoy, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Mathilde de Garlande. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1202. Ele faleceu em 1204, em İstanbul, İstanbul, Império Otomano, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em İstanbul, Turquia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III родился в 1135 г., его отцу по имени Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали 'Alice' FitzRoy, 28. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Mathilde de Garlande. В 1202 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1204 г. в местности истанбул, истанбул, Османская империя в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности истанбул, Турция.

sm.from 1202 to 1204: mai le 1202 e o'o I le 1204

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mattheus Van Montmorency Heer Van Marly III nació en 1135, su papá, Mathieu de Montmorency I  Seigneur de Montmorency, tenía 46 años y su mamá, 'Alice' FitzRoy, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Mathilde de Garlande. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1202. Murió en 1204, en İstanbul, İstanbul, Imperio otomano, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en İstanbul, Turquía.
